nodes.name: Budget Owner reviews commitment vs available budget
nodes.documentation: Budget Owner (permission budget.approve, scope = own cost-center) reviews the requisition value against the cost-center / project budget for the period. Displayed read-only fields: costCenterId, period, budget.amount, budget.availableAmount, budget.committedAmount, budget.actualAmount, this record's totalAmountInBase, projected available-after-commit = availableAmount - totalAmountInBase. Action buttons: 'Approve budget' (effect: BudgetDecisionGate TRUE branch, records budgetApprovedBy + budgetApprovedAt, completion -> APPROVED), 'Approve override' (effect: same APPROVED path but sets budget.overrideFlag = true and requires mandatory field overrideReason [text, mandatory when availableAmount < totalAmountInBase], logged to audit), 'Return for revision' (effect: BudgetDecisionGate FALSE branch, mandatory field returnNote [text]). Note: this is the SOFT check at requisition stage; the HARD commit/encumbrance happens later at PO issue (Diagram 05), not here. Owning role: Budget Owner. [SCOR OE11 | source: build-new budget service + AB over-budget override | platform-services 11]

nodes.name: Approver reviews requisition
nodes.documentation: The resolved approver (approvalRequestedToId user, OR an assignee of sufficient designation rank) opens the record. Authorization (service-layer, on top of the permission guard): the acting user is the approvalRequestedToId OR an assignee with required DESIGNATION_RANK; AND the SoD rule holds (acting user is NOT the record's requester for a requisition, NOT the buyer for a PO - never approve your own record). On open, completion moves IN_PROGRESS -> AWAITING_APPROVAL if request-approval has fired; the approver sees the full record (header, lines, totals in record currency and base currency, attachments, prior-stage decisions, prior return notes). Read-only here; the decision is taken at the ApproveDecision gateway via the action buttons. Displayed action buttons: 'Approve' and 'Return for revision' (with mandatory note). Owning role: Approver (permission approval.approve, conditional per role-permission-matrix). [SCOR OE2 | ISO 8.4 | source: RA approve authorization + SoD | AB FM Approve/Reject/Return]

nodes.name: Revise and resubmit (same record, identifier unchanged)
nodes.documentation: The record owner (Requester for a requisition, Buyer for a PO) edits the SAME record per the return note; the record identifier is immutable and unchanged across the revision (AB rule: identifier persists across delays and revisions). Editable fields depend on recordType and the field engine (mandatory-field gating by stage/scope/purchaseType). Effect of resubmit: the record returns to the approval flow at the FIRST stage (re-enters AssignStage / re-evaluation) so changed financial information is re-checked. Note the financial-revert rule (see FinancialEditRevert): if payment terms or payment schedules changed, any auto-approved Finance/Management completions are reverted to AWAITING_APPROVAL on resubmit. Owning role: Requester (requisition.edit, requisition.submit) or Buyer (po.edit). [SCOR OE2 | source: RA edit-same-record + AB return-for-revision loop, identifier immutable]

nodes.name: Resolve approval chain (routing rule by department / category / value)
nodes.documentation: System resolves the ordered approval chain for this record. Lookup key: RoutingRuleEntry matched on (departmentId, category, recordType) with a value band; unique constraint (department, stage/verticalId). Default chain template (configurable per tenant, fixed order): Stage 1 REQ_DEPARTMENT (assignee = requester, pinned) -> Stage 2 PROCUREMENT -> Stage 3 BUDGET (conditional, see BudgetStageGate) -> Stage 4 FINANCE -> Stage 5 MANAGEMENT -> null (terminal). Al Bahja 'all-to-one' (single FM/PurchaseHead) and Raphe 'multi-stage' are both expressed as routing-rule configurations of this one engine; a site/department-conditional stage models AB 'factory site needs Factory Manager' (FM sites: Barka Engineering, Oman Hygienic Stores, Oman Hygienic Engineering; FM-skip sites: Oman Solar, Accommodation Maintenance, Flavour Division, Photon-01). Each RoutingRuleEntry fields: departmentId (mandatory), verticalId/stage (mandatory), assigneeUserIds[] (optional), approverUserIds[] (mandatory), valueBandMin/valueBandMax (optional, default unbounded). If no routing rule matches: BadRequestException 'No routing rule', record stalls IN_PROGRESS until an Administrator (routingRules.update) fixes the rule. Owning role: Platform/System (automated); configuration owned by Administrator (approval.configureChain). [SCOR OE2 | ISO 8.4 | source: RA routing strategies + AB FM-site config | platform-services 1]

nodes.name: Create per-stage completions (NOT_STARTED)
nodes.documentation: System creates one VerticalCompletion record per resolved stage, each initialized status = NOT_STARTED. Completion fields: completionId, recordId, verticalId/stage, status {NOT_STARTED|IN_PROGRESS|AWAITING_APPROVAL|APPROVED}, assignedToId (nullable), approvalRequestedToId (nullable), approvalLimit (resolved per assigned approver, nullable), isAutoApproved (boolean, default false), approvedAt (nullable timestamp), revertedCount (integer, default 0). The three-dimension state model (RA): record STAGE (INITIATION->ORDERED->...) is independent of per-stage COMPLETION; this diagram drives COMPLETION only. Completions are created in chain order; index 0 is the first stage to action. Owning role: Platform/System. [SCOR OE2 | ISO 8.4 | source: RA three-dimension completion model | data-model ApprovalChain/VerticalCompletion]

nodes.name: Assign current stage (least-loaded eligible approver)
nodes.documentation: System assigns the current (first not-yet-APPROVED) stage. Stage 1 REQ_DEPARTMENT assignee is pinned to the requester; the stage approver and every later-stage assignee/approver are selected by least-loaded among the eligible pool from the routing rule. Eligibility pool = active users (status ACTIVE) in approverUserIds[] for the stage who satisfy the stage permission (approval.approve) and the SoD rule (userId != record.requesterId for a requisition, userId != record.buyerId for a PO). Least-loaded metric = urgency-weighted count of the user's open (non-APPROVED) completions across both assignedToId and approvalRequestedToId; urgency weights ASAP=4, SameDay=2, Within2Days=1, Within1Week=0; lowest weighted count wins, ties broken by lowest userId. First assignment moves the completion NOT_STARTED -> IN_PROGRESS and emits NOTIFY_ASSIGNED (templated email) + SSE {recordId}. Guard on a manual reassignment (approval.reassign): assigner designation rank >= the stage's required rank (DESIGNATION_RANK) and the completion is not yet APPROVED. Owning role: Platform/System (auto) / Approver or Administrator (manual reassign). [SCOR OE2 | ISO 8.4 | source: RA findLeastLoadedUser urgency-weighted + assignment guard | platform-services 1]

nodes.name: Auto-approve (nearest-bucket minimum-sufficient approver, logged)
nodes.documentation: System auto-approves the stage without a human action. Nearest-bucket selection picks the minimum-sufficient approver so high-limit approvers are reserved for large amounts: (1) load active eligible approvers for the stage; (2) effectiveLimit = approvalLimit ?? 200000 (configured 0 stays 0); (3) keep only those with effectiveLimit >= totalAmountInBase (eligible, inclusive); (4) NEAREST BUCKET = retain only the approvers at the MINIMUM eligible limit; (5) tie-break by least-loaded (urgency-weighted open-completion count). Effects: completion.status = APPROVED, isAutoApproved = true, approvedAt = now, approvalRequestedToId = the bucket approver (for audit), NO approval-requested email is sent, audit log entry 'AUTO_APPROVED' with the limit and amount. Auto-approval does NOT run the human OTIF/user-stats path and does NOT by itself complete the record. Empty pool at step 1 or step 3 -> BadRequestException 'No eligible approver', transaction rolls back, record stalls. Owning role: Platform/System. [SCOR OE2 | source: RA nearest-bucket auto-approval | raphe-routing-approval e01 | platform-services 1]

nodes.name: Record stage APPROVED (manual or auto), update stats
nodes.documentation: System commits the stage decision in a transaction: completion.status = APPROVED, approvedAt = now, approvedById = acting approver (for manual) or null (for auto, isAutoApproved already true). For a MANUAL approval only (not auto): update the assignee's and approver's OTIF / user-stats streak - remainingTime = allowedDuration(priority) - elapsed(record.createdAt); <=0 = MISS (resets currentStreak to 0), >0 = HIT (same-day upgrades MISS->HIT once, new-day increments; longestStreak is monotonic; totals always accumulate; otifPercentage = totalHits/(totalHits+totalMisses)*100). Emits NOTIFY_STAGE_APPROVED (templated email) + SSE {recordId} + audit 'STAGE_APPROVED'. Owning role: Platform/System. [SCOR OE2 | ISO 8.4 | source: RA approve transaction + e07 OTIF streak | platform-services 4/6/9]

nodes.name: Advance to next stage (handleRouting)
nodes.documentation: System advances the chain: set the next not-APPROVED completion as current, resolve its approver per that stage's routing strategy (Procurement: assignee from rule, least-loaded, no auto-approver; Finance: approval-only, approver = assignee via the finance nearest-bucket selection; Management/default: both from rule, least-loaded), then re-enter the assignment + threshold loop. If the next stage is auto-approvable and within limit, the auto-approval path recurses to the following stage (e.g. Finance auto-approves then routes to Management which always requires a human). Loops back to AssignStage. Owning role: Platform/System. [SCOR OE2 | source: RA per-vertical routing strategies + recurse-on-auto | raphe-routing-approval]

nodes.name: Record fully approved, release to next domain
nodes.documentation: All completions APPROVED. System sets the record's approval state to FULLY_APPROVED and enables the next domain action (a requisition becomes ready for sourcing in Diagram 04; a PO becomes ready to issue in Diagram 05; an award becomes ready to award; a payment schedule becomes ready to release). Emits NOTIFY_FULLY_APPROVED + SSE {recordId} + audit 'FULLY_APPROVED'. Note: full approval is NOT record completion; the record STATUS reaches COMPLETED only at end-of-cycle (POST_DELIVERY + all matches cleared + no open NCR/CAPA). Owning role: Platform/System. [SCOR OE2 | ISO 8.4 | source: RA chain terminal + three-dimension model]

nodes.name: Revert auto-approved Finance / Management completions
nodes.documentation: Auto-approval REVERT (RA postValueSync e02). After any write to the record the system recomputes total = sum(qty x unitPrice) and reconverts to base currency if it changed; THEN, only if payTerms OR paymentSchedules changed, it reverts every completion where (verticalId IN {FINANCE, MANAGEMENT}) AND (status == APPROVED) AND (isAutoApproved == true) back to AWAITING_APPROVAL, increments revertedCount, and re-queues those stages for decision. Manual approvals are NEVER reverted; REQ_DEPARTMENT and PROCUREMENT completions are never reverted. The hook always emits 'record.updated' (SSE) and never aborts the underlying write. This prevents a financial change from riding through on a stale auto-approval. After revert, the reverted stage re-enters the threshold evaluation. Loops back to AutoApprovalGate. Owning role: Platform/System. [SCOR OE2 | source: RA e02 postValueSync auto-approval revert | platform-services 1]

nodes.name: Is current stage the Budget stage (configured)?
nodes.documentation: Branch expression: currentStage.verticalId == 'BUDGET' && tenantConfig.budgetStageEnabled == true. The budget gate is a configurable stage in the chain, not a hard-coded step. TRUE -> route to the Budget Owner for an explicit budget decision (BudgetReview). FALSE -> route to the threshold evaluation for a normal approver stage (AutoApprovalGate). If the budget stage is disabled for the tenant, ResolveChain never creates it, so this gateway only ever sees FALSE in that configuration. Owning role: Platform/System. [SCOR OE11 | source: build-new finance control as configurable approval stage | platform-services 11]

nodes.name: Budget approved or override approved?
nodes.documentation: Branch expression: budgetDecision == 'APPROVE' || budgetDecision == 'OVERRIDE'. TRUE (within available budget, i.e. totalAmountInBase <= budget.availableAmount, OR override explicitly approved with overrideReason) -> RecordStageApproved (this stage is done, advance the chain). FALSE (returnNote supplied, no override) -> ReturnForRevision. Owning role: Budget Owner. [SCOR OE11 | source: build-new]

nodes.name: Within auto-approval threshold?
nodes.documentation: Branch expression: stage.allowAutoApproval == true && totalAmountInBase <= effectiveApproverLimit, where effectiveApproverLimit = (assignedApprover.approvalLimit ?? DEFAULT_LIMIT) and DEFAULT_LIMIT = 200000 (configurable per tenant, a configured value of 0 stays 0, never falls back to 200000). Comparison is INCLUSIVE: an amount exactly equal to the limit auto-approves (<=). allowAutoApproval is true only for stages flagged auto-approvable in config (RA: Finance stage; AB single-stage tenants may flag none). TRUE -> AutoApprove. FALSE -> ManualReview. The limit used for the auto-approval decision is taken from the stage's resolved approver (approvalRequestedTo ?? assignedTo); note this can diverge from the per-candidate eligibility limit used during nearest-bucket selection (a known Raphe behaviour, documented not changed). Owning role: Platform/System. [SCOR OE2 | source: RA allowAutoApproval guard + nearest-bucket | raphe-routing-approval | platform-services 1]

nodes.name: Approve or return for revision?
nodes.documentation: Branch expression: approverDecision == 'APPROVE'. TRUE -> RecordStageApproved. FALSE (approverDecision == 'RETURN', requires mandatory field returnNote [text, mandatory]) -> ReturnForRevision. The 'Approve' button is permission-gated (approval.approve) AND SoD-gated (actingUserId != record.requesterId and != record.buyerId); if SoD fails the button is disabled with reason 'Cannot approve your own record'. The 'Return for revision' button requires returnNote before it submits. Owning role: Approver. [SCOR OE2 | source: RA approve/reject + AB reject-reason-mandatory]

nodes.name: More stages remaining in chain?
nodes.documentation: Branch expression: exists(completion where status != 'APPROVED'). TRUE (at least one later stage is still not APPROVED) -> AdvanceStage (handleRouting to the next stage). FALSE (every completion in the chain is APPROVED) -> FullyApproved. Management is the terminal stage and never auto-approves; the chain completes only when its completion is APPROVED. Owning role: Platform/System. [SCOR OE2 | source: RA handleRouting to next vertical, Management terminal | raphe-routing-approval]

nodes.name: Requisition submitted for approval
nodes.documentation: Trigger: a requester submits a requisition (or, reusing the same engine, a buyer submits a PO, award, supplier-onboarding, or payment-schedule record). The submitted record carries: recordType {Requisition|PurchaseOrder|Award|Supplier|PaymentSchedule}, recordId, requesterId (owner, immutable), departmentId, category {Items|Spares|Services|ProductDesign}, purchaseType {Local|Import} or {Direct|Indirect}, currency, totalAmount (record currency), totalAmountInBase (FX-normalized by the FX service, the value all thresholds compare against), projectId/costCenterId, priority {ASAP|SameDay|Within2Days|Within1Week}. Owning role for submit action: Requester (requisition.submit) or Buyer (po.issue precursor). On submit the record status moves IN_PROGRESS and is locked for structural edits except via return-for-revision. [SCOR OE2 | ISO 8.4 | source: RA submit + AB requisition-status]

nodes.name: Approval chain complete
nodes.documentation: Terminal node of the approval workflow for this record. The record proceeds to its downstream domain diagram. [SCOR OE2 | source: build-new]

nodes.name: Financial info changed
nodes.documentation: Non-interrupting boundary event. Condition: an edit to the record changed payTerms OR paymentSchedules (postValueSync hook). On fire, the revert path runs without cancelling the main flow. [SCOR OE2 | source: RA postValueSync e02]
